text: The surgeon performs minimal dissection to create space between the right colon, right liver, and abdominal wall. A large piece of coated polypropylene mesh is inserted into the abdomen for bridging repair. The mesh is oriented correctly, and several stay sutures are placed circumferentially to anchor the mesh to the abdominal wall.

The surgeon uses a running self-retaining absorbable suture to secure the mesh circumferentially, starting from the posterior location and running sutures in both directions. After the mesh is fully sewn in place, it is inspected, and additional anchoring sutures are placed to prevent displacement or migration.
